# Daily Life

## Purpose
The texture of ordinary daily life — how someone spends the twenty-four hours they are given each day — is among the most grounded and concrete sources of understanding available. Abstract psychology becomes visible in daily patterns: when someone wakes, how they begin their day, what they do with discretionary time, how they manage obligations, and how they end the day all reveal priorities, rhythms, struggles, and sources of satisfaction that broader narratives can miss.

## What to capture here
- Typical wake time and how they greet the morning
- Morning routine and its psychological function
- Primary occupation of working hours on a typical day
- How they spend discretionary time — the hours that are theirs to choose
- Social contact on a typical day: who they interact with and in what ways
- Digital life on a typical day: how much screen time, what platforms, what for
- Physical activity or movement during the day
- Meals: when, what, with whom, and in what spirit
- Evening routine and wind-down practices
- Sleep habits and typical bedtime
- The quality they most want their daily life to have that it currently lacks
- The thing they do every day that brings the most reliable satisfaction
- How their current daily life compares to the life they imagined for themselves
- What a truly good day looks like for them from beginning to end

## Notes
